source: Extracted from Winedr app wineCards.ts. Supplemental wines scored from wine knowledge.
dimensions_scale: 1-10 (app wines converted from 0-100 stats; supplemental wines scored directly)

wines.name: Beaujolais
wines.pairing_note: The high acidity and light body make this the ultimate food-friendly red wine - pair with anything

wines.name: Cinsault
wines.pairing_note: Silky and fresh — excellent slightly chilled with charcuterie, grilled vegetables, and casual summer meals.

wines.name: Etna Rosso
wines.pairing_note: Volcanic minerality and high acidity work beautifully with umami-rich dishes and delicate proteins

wines.name: Frappato
wines.pairing_note: Delicate red fruit and floral lift pair beautifully with light Italian food and grilled fish

wines.name: Gamay de Touraine
wines.pairing_note: Crunchy red fruit and bright acidity make this the perfect casual food wine — works with almost anything light

wines.name: Mencía
wines.pairing_note: Floral and mineral with a food-friendly structure — great with roast chicken, pork, and tapas.

wines.name: Morgon
wines.pairing_note: Earthy depth and dark cherry fruit bridge the gap between light reds and more structured wines

wines.name: Oregon Pinot Noir
wines.pairing_note: Silky texture and earthy notes work beautifully with delicate proteins like salmon and duck

wines.name: Red Burgundy
wines.pairing_note: Silk tannins and earthy depth pair beautifully with duck, salmon, and any dish where subtlety matters

wines.name: Valpolicella
wines.pairing_note: Light and tart with sour cherry fruit — perfect with pizza, pasta, and Italian-American food.

wines.name: Barbera
wines.pairing_note: High acidity and bright fruit cut through rich tomato sauces and fatty Italian dishes

wines.name: Barolo
wines.pairing_note: Huge tannins and complex flavors need rich fatty foods like aged beef and truffle dishes

wines.name: Chianti Classico
wines.pairing_note: Made to pair with tomato-based Italian food - the high acidity cuts right through rich red sauce

wines.name: Cotes du Rhone
wines.pairing_note: Versatile and balanced enough to pair with everything from roast chicken to cheese

wines.name: Dolcetto
wines.pairing_note: Low acid and soft tannins make it unusually food-flexible — great with pizza, antipasto, and casual Italian meals.

wines.name: Grenache
wines.pairing_note: Juicy ripe fruit and warm spice pair beautifully with grilled foods and Mediterranean flavors

wines.name: Malbec
wines.pairing_note: Fruit forward and smooth with enough structure to stand up to grilled meats and bold flavors

wines.name: Merlot
wines.pairing_note: Plush tannins and dark fruit pair well with roasted meats, mushroom dishes, and aged cheeses.

wines.name: Montepulciano
wines.pairing_note: Juicy fruit and bright acidity pair perfectly with tomato sauce and casual Italian food

wines.name: Mourvèdre
wines.pairing_note: Gamey and meaty with firm tannins — built for lamb, wild game, and hearty stews.

wines.name: Napa Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.pairing_note: Big tannins need fat and protein to shine - think rich steaks, aged cheese, or anything grilled

wines.name: Nero d'Avola
wines.pairing_note: Bold dark fruit and warm spice pair well with grilled lamb, pasta with rich meat sauce, and aged cheeses.

wines.name: Pinotage
wines.pairing_note: Smoky and fruit-forward — pairs well with BBQ, grilled meats, and anything off the fire.

wines.name: Primitivo
wines.pairing_note: Rich and jammy with a warming finish — built for grilled meats, hearty pasta, and strong cheeses.

wines.name: Rioja
wines.pairing_note: Oak and savory notes pair beautifully with aged meats, lamb, and Spanish tapas

wines.name: Ruby Port
wines.pairing_note: Dark fruit and rich sweetness stand up to bold desserts and strong cheese — the classic chocolate pairing

wines.name: Syrah/Shiraz
wines.pairing_note: Smoky peppery notes and dark fruit pair perfectly with grilled meats and bold flavors

wines.name: Tawny Port
wines.pairing_note: Oxidative aging creates walnut and caramel notes — extraordinary with blue cheese or alone after dinner

wines.name: Tempranillo
wines.pairing_note: Earthy savory notes and medium body pair beautifully with cured meats and traditional Spanish dishes

wines.name: Zinfandel
wines.pairing_note: Bold jammy fruit and high alcohol pair with bold flavors like BBQ sauce and grilled meats

wines.name: Bandol Rosé
wines.pairing_note: Mourvèdre backbone gives it structure to match rich Provençal dishes and seafood stews

wines.name: Cerasuolo d'Abruzzo
wines.pairing_note: Bright cherry fruit and good acidity make this a natural pizza and pasta companion

wines.name: Malbec Rosé
wines.pairing_note: Plummy Malbec fruit survives the rosé process — excellent with grilled meats

wines.name: Nebbiolo Rosato
wines.pairing_note: Nebbiolo's signature tannins persist even in rosé — it wants food, preferably Northern Italian

wines.name: Pinot Noir Rosé
wines.pairing_note: The delicate, mineral style pairs beautifully with lighter fare — sushi, salads, fresh cheeses

wines.name: Provence Rosé
wines.pairing_note: Crisp and dry with bright acidity that cuts through rich or oily foods while staying light and refreshing

wines.name: Sangiovese Rosato
wines.pairing_note: Sangiovese's natural savory character shines in rosé form — perfect with Italian food

wines.name: Tavel Rosé
wines.pairing_note: The fullest, most food-serious rosé in France — built for the table, not the pool deck

wines.name: White Zinfandel
wines.pairing_note: Off-dry sweetness makes it surprisingly food-friendly — great with BBQ, pizza, or anything spicy

wines.name: Albarino
wines.pairing_note: Saline mineral notes and citrus acidity match ocean flavors perfectly - born for shellfish

wines.name: Assyrtiko
wines.pairing_note: Electric acidity and saline minerality mirror the sea — born to drink with anything pulled from the ocean

wines.name: Chablis
wines.pairing_note: Striking mineral notes and bright acidity mirror the taste of the sea - perfect with shellfish

wines.name: Chenin Blanc
wines.pairing_note: Touch of sweetness and honeyed texture balance spicy foods while high acidity keeps it fresh

wines.name: Dry Gewurztraminer
wines.pairing_note: Bold aromatics and slight spice cut through rich fatty dishes and complement bold Asian flavors

wines.name: Dry Muscat
wines.pairing_note: Grapey freshness and floral lift make this a natural aperitif — light enough for delicate dishes

wines.name: Dry Riesling
wines.pairing_note: Razor-sharp acidity and mineral backbone cut through rich foods while complementing delicate flavors

wines.name: Gruner Veltliner
wines.pairing_note: Spicy peppery notes and high acidity work with bold flavors that would overwhelm other whites

wines.name: Late Harvest Riesling
wines.pairing_note: Riesling's electric acidity keeps even extreme sweetness tasting fresh and lively

wines.name: Malmsey Madeira
wines.pairing_note: Madeira's volcanic terroir and unique estufagem process creates wine that literally cannot go bad once opened

wines.name: Moscato
wines.pairing_note: Sweet fruity wine pairs beautifully with fruit desserts or sip on its own as dessert

wines.name: New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc
wines.pairing_note: Electric acidity and bold citrus flavors pair beautifully with fresh Asian flavors and salads

wines.name: Oaked Chardonnay
wines.pairing_note: Butter and vanilla from oak aging coat rich proteins beautifully — the classic white with rich food

wines.name: Sancerre
wines.pairing_note: Bright acidity and herbal notes pair beautifully with fresh green flavors and goat cheese

wines.name: Sauternes
wines.pairing_note: Noble rot concentrates the grapes to extraordinary complexity — acidity balances the intense sweetness

wines.name: Tokaji Aszú
wines.pairing_note: Called 'wine of kings' since the 17th century — extraordinary acidity keeps extreme sweetness alive

wines.name: Vermentino
wines.pairing_note: Herbal citrus notes and bright acidity pair beautifully with fresh vegetables and seafood

wines.name: Vinho Verde
wines.pairing_note: Light fizz and low alcohol pair perfectly with light foods without weighing you down

wines.name: Viognier
wines.pairing_note: Lush texture and exotic aromatics pair beautifully with rich foods and bold spicy flavors

wines.name: Vouvray
wines.pairing_note: Touch of honey and rich texture balance spice while high acidity keeps it food friendly

wines.name: White Rioja
wines.pairing_note: Oak aging adds texture and nuttiness that bridges between light and rich whites — excellent with shellfish

wines.name: Asti Spumante
wines.pairing_note: Fully sparkling and very sweet — made for cake, panettone, and festive occasions

wines.name: Blanc de Blancs Champagne
wines.pairing_note: Pure Chardonnay Champagne — all chalk, lemon, and toasted bread. The highest expression of mineral sparkling

wines.name: Brachetto d'Acqui
wines.pairing_note: The only wine that truly pairs with chocolate — strawberry and rose notes bridge the gap

wines.name: Brut Nature Cava
wines.pairing_note: No added sugar lets the earthy, toasty character shine through — ideal with savory Spanish bites

wines.name: Bugey-Cerdon
wines.pairing_note: Fizzy pink semi-sweet Gamay — Beaujolais and Brachetto had a French baby

wines.name: Cava Brut
wines.pairing_note: Brut Cava is one of the world's best values in sparkling wine — biscuity and food-friendly

wines.name: Cava Semi-Seco
wines.pairing_note: Spanish bubbles with gentle sweetness — the affordable crowd-pleaser for dessert toasts

wines.name: Champagne
wines.pairing_note: High acidity and elegant bubbles cut through rich foods while elevating any moment to special

wines.name: Crémant d'Alsace
wines.pairing_note: Alsatian aromatics make this more floral and expressive than other Crémants — great aperitif wine

wines.name: Crémant de Bourgogne
wines.pairing_note: Burgundian grapes with traditional method — Champagne elegance at a fraction of the cost

wines.name: Crémant Demi-Sec
wines.pairing_note: Loire Chenin Blanc's honey and floral notes bloom with a touch of sweetness

wines.name: Demi-Sec Champagne
wines.pairing_note: Champagne's signature acidity keeps the sweetness lifted — perfect with fruit desserts

wines.name: Frizzante Soave
wines.pairing_note: Garganega's natural almond note and light bubbles make this the ultimate Italian aperitivo

wines.name: Lambrusco
wines.pairing_note: Bubbles and acidity cut through rich fatty foods while fruit keeps it fun and approachable

wines.name: Moscato d'Asti
wines.pairing_note: Light frizzante sweetness with only 5-6% alcohol — the original brunch wine and perfect dessert pairing

wines.name: Pét-Nat
wines.pairing_note: Low-intervention sparkling with slight fizz — hazy, textural, and uniquely alive

wines.name: Prosecco
wines.pairing_note: Light bubbles and touch of sweetness pair beautifully with brunch foods and salty appetizers

wines.name: Sparkling Shiraz
wines.pairing_note: Bold Shiraz with bubbles — Australia's most festive party trick. Especially beloved at Christmas

wines.name: Amber Pinot Grigio
wines.pairing_note: Light skin contact adds texture without overwhelming — the accessible entry point to orange wine

wines.name: Georgian Mtsvane
wines.pairing_note: More floral than Rkatsiteli but equally tannic — traditional Georgian skin-contact at its most aromatic

wines.name: Georgian Rkatsiteli
wines.pairing_note: Extended qvevri skin contact produces serious tannins and walnut depth — a natural wine landmark

wines.name: Long-Maceration Amber
wines.pairing_note: Extended maceration creates white wine with red wine tannins — extraordinary with rich savory food

wines.name: Ramato Pinot Grigio
wines.pairing_note: Short skin contact gives copper color, tannic grip, and savory depth — the original Pinot Grigio

wines.name: Skin-Contact Pinot Gris
wines.pairing_note: Extended skin contact amplifies Pinot Gris's natural spice and texture — extraordinary with rich food
